title: Bloom Energy Shares Slide Friday: What's Going On?
text: Bloom Energy shares were down 7.61% on Friday as traders reassessed the stock's post-earnings rally in a risk-off tape. The article indicates that broader market caution is driving the pullback rather than company-specific negative news. Positive catalysts include strong Q1 earnings beat with adjusted EPS of 44 cents versus 13 cents expected, raised fiscal 2026 guidance to $751.05M revenue versus $551.55M expected, 208% increase in product revenue, and operating cash flow improvement of $184.3M year-over-year. The stock exhibits a classic high-flyer setup with exceptional momentum and growth metrics paired with weak valuation fundamentals, making it vulnerable to sharp pullbacks during risk-off market environments. BE trades significantly stretched from its moving averages, 6.9% above its 20-day moving average and 121.7% above its 200-day moving average. The MACD indicator shows weakening upside momentum, though the golden-cross moving average structure remains bullish. Key support is at $249, with resistance near $303.
